stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a significant LLM release.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
Indicates the model is relevant to text-generation tasks within the NLP domain.
Tags confirm its use of standard ML frameworks like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is committing massive investment ($1T) to bolster its semiconductor and memory chip production capabilities.
The national strategy explicitly targets achieving a leadership position in physical AI.
A key goal is the commercial deployment of humanoid robots by the year 2028.

stories.title: Scaling the Horizon, Not the Parameters: Reaching Trillion-Parameter Performance with a 35B Agent
stories.author: Yuhao Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces Agents-A1, a 35B Mixture-of-Experts Agentic Model.
Achieves performance comparable to trillion-parameter models by scaling the 'agent horizon' rather than model size.
Utilizes a long-horizon knowledge-action infrastructure to manage complex, long-trajectory tasks (average length of 45K steps).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, suggesting a modern LLM architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon devices.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher quality model outputs, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new AI model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents run locally on the user's own hardware, emphasizing privacy and control.
It features built-in support for Ollama, leveraging existing local LLM infrastructure.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for optimized performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in preview, signaling a push for hardware-accelerated local AI.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting external communication tools to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Xbox weighs canceling Blade game and shuttering Arkane
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is planning significant layoffs across its Xbox studios.
The restructuring may involve closing at least five studios, potential mergers, and game cancellations.
The news signals a major strategic shift and cost-cutting measure within Microsoft's gaming division.

stories.title: US offers $10 million for info on group behind Signal and WhatsApp hacking spree
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: US government is actively seeking information regarding a hacking campaign targeting Signal and WhatsApp.
The operation is attributed to two distinct Russia-state-sponsored groups.
The hacking spree has reportedly been active for at least since March.

stories.title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
stories.author: Yang Deng
bullet_points: Introduces WorldEvolver, a framework designed to enhance LLM agent planning using self-evolving world models.
Addresses the critical issue of unreliable foresight in world models by allowing the model to revise its deployment-time context.
Maintains a frozen downstream agent and model parameters while integrating modules like Episodic Memory for improved decision-making.

stories.title: Words Speak Louder Than Code: Investigating Cognitive Heuristics in LLM-Based Code Vulnerability Detection
stories.author: Z. Berkay Celik
bullet_points: Investigates the impact of human cognitive heuristics on LLMs used for code vulnerability detection.
Addresses a gap in research by systematically exploring how biases affect LLM security assessments.
Introduces a controlled framework to study the relationship between cognitive biases and LLM performance in code analysis.

stories.title: One-Step Gradient Delay is Not a Barrier for Large-Scale Asynchronous Pipeline Parallel LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Samuel Horváth
bullet_points: Addresses a key bottleneck in large-scale LLM pretraining: GPU idle time caused by pipeline bubbles in synchronous parallelism.
Introduces asynchronous pipeline parallelism as a method to maximize throughput by accepting gradient staleness.
Presents PipeDream-2BW as an advanced asynchronous schedule that maintains a constant one-step gradient delay, improving upon previous methods.

stories.title: SWE-INTERACT: Reimagining SWE Benchmarks as User-Driven Long-Horizon Coding Sessions
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: Introduces SWE-Interact, a new testbed for evaluating coding AI agents.
Shifts evaluation from autonomous, upfront-defined tasks to realistic, multi-turn, user-driven workflows.
Simulates a real developer experience where requirements are vague or revealed progressively by a user simulator.

stories.title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
stories.author: Yang Deng
bullet_points: Introduces WorldEvolver, a framework for self-evolving world models to enhance LLM agent planning.
Addresses the issue of unreliable foresight in LLM agents by allowing the model to revise its deployment-time context.
The framework maintains frozen downstream agent and model parameters while integrating modules like Episodic Memory.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces significant performance improvements.
Enhanced GGUF model compatibility is achieved via llama.cpp integration.
The update broadens model support across various hardware, augmenting the MLX engine on Apple silicon.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for more complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to include local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google's NotebookLM is introducing a new feature to generate short, TikTok-style AI videos from user-uploaded research sources.
The feature is currently exclusive to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers.
This represents an evolution in how AI tools summarize complex information, moving towards highly digestible, short-form video content.

stories.title: Netflix is using an AI-generated Gene Wilder voice in its Willy Wonka reality show
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Netflix is utilizing AI-generated voice technology for its upcoming Willy Wonka reality show, 'Wonka's The Golden Ticket'.
The production features physical, real sets, distinguishing it from purely AI-generated visual content.
The use of AI voiceover highlights the increasing integration of generative AI into mainstream entertainment production.

stories.title: Rockstar workers push to unionize ahead of GTA VI’s launch
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Rockstar Games employees are actively pushing for union recognition via the IWGB Game Workers Union.
The unionization effort comes in the wake of Rockstar firing over 30 staff members last year.
The firings have been publicly accused by workers of being a 'union busting' tactic.

stories.title: The US going 100% EV by 2040 would save more than 100k lives, study says
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: A study suggests a 100% EV transition in the US by 2040 could prevent over 100,000 deaths.
A significant portion of the projected health benefits stems from electrifying heavy-duty vehicles like trucks and buses.
The news highlights the intersection of climate policy, transportation infrastructure, and public health outcomes.

stories.title: US renewable boom passes key milestone in April
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: US renewable energy generation has reached a significant milestone.
Small-scale solar power played a crucial role in this growth.
Renewables now generate nearly triple the amount of power produced by coal in the US.

stories.title: VLK: Learning Humanoid Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Interactions in Reconstructed Scenes
stories.author: Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the critical data bottleneck in humanoid robotics by generating synthetic Vision-Language-Kinematics (VLK) supervision.
The method utilizes 3D Gaussian Splatting within reconstructed scenes to create rich, synchronized training data.
Aims to connect egocentric observations and language commands directly to complex, whole-body motion for humanoid manipulation.

stories.title: LeVo 2: Stable and Melodious Song Generation via Hierarchical Representation Modeling and Progressive Post-Training
stories.author: Dong Yu
bullet_points: LeVo 2 introduces a hybrid LLM-Diffusion framework for generating full-length, coherent, and melodious songs.
It addresses the structural trade-off in existing systems between preserving vocal-instrument coordination and maintaining detailed track acoustics.
The model is designed for controllable generation, ensuring adherence to lyrics and user prompts.

stories.title: GROW$^2$: Grounding Which and Where for Robot Tool Use
stories.author: David Hsu
bullet_points: The research addresses the 'open-world affordance grounding' challenge in robotics, allowing robots to use tools creatively beyond their intended function.
GROW$^2$ (GROunding Which and Where) is introduced to solve this by leveraging object parts as a hierarchical abstraction for grounding.
The work focuses on enabling robots to select and localize specific regions of action on open-category objects (e.g., using a plate to cut a cake).

stories.title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
stories.author: Divya Chaudhary
bullet_points: The paper challenges the common assumption that conservative offline training prevents reward hacking during online adaptation.
It empirically and mechanistically investigates how different levels of conservatism ($\beta$) affect policy behavior in reasoning models.
The research uses a Qwen3-14B policy trained with Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) as a case study.

stories.title: C$^{2}$R: Cross-sample Consistency Regularization Mitigates Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Defu Lian
bullet_points: The paper addresses critical limitations in Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) when applied to large language models (LLMs).
Key problems identified are 'feature splitting' (fragmentation of concepts) and 'feature absorption' (creation of arbitrary exceptions).
The proposed solution, C^2R (Cross-sample Consistency Regularization), aims to improve latent reliability in SAEs.

stories.title: MESA: Prioritizing Vulnerable Communication Channels for Securing Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Patrick McDaniel
bullet_points: Addresses security vulnerabilities in Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) communication channels.
Proposes a method (MESA) for defenders to prioritize limited security resources.
Highlights that the impact of channel-level attacks in MAS is highly non-uniform.

stories.title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
stories.author: Divya Chaudhary
bullet_points: The paper challenges the common belief that conservative offline training inherently prevents reward hacking during online adaptation.
Researchers empirically and mechanistically investigate how different levels of conservatism ($\beta$) affect policy behavior in reasoning models (using Qwen3-14B and DPO).
The study suggests that overly conservative approaches might inadvertently amplify reward hacking during the fine-tuning phase.

stories.title: C$^{2}$R: Cross-sample Consistency Regularization Mitigates Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Defu Lian
bullet_points: The paper addresses critical limitations in Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) when applied to large language models (LLMs).
Key problems identified are 'feature splitting' (fragmentation of concepts) and 'feature absorption' (creation of arbitrary exceptions).
The proposed solution, C²R (Cross-sample Consistency Regularization), aims to improve latent reliability in SAEs.

stories.title: A Hybrid Framework For Crypto-Ransomware Detection In Enterprise Shared Storage
stories.author: Mohammad Jabed Morshed Chowdhury
bullet_points: The paper addresses the evolving threat of crypto-ransomware targeting enterprise shared storage, moving beyond traditional endpoint focus.
Traditional detection methods are insufficient because compromised client endpoints can encrypt remote shared data without triggering local alerts.
The research proposes a 'Hybrid Framework' specifically designed to enhance detection capabilities against these network-based ransomware attacks.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Generation and Decision-Making Under Ambiguity
stories.author: Iryna Gurevych
bullet_points: Addresses the critical need for trust and reliability in LLM outputs for complex, subjective real-world tasks.
Focuses on uncertainty-aware decision-making algorithms, an area less explored than model training itself.
Presents and evaluates novel methods to help systems make decisions when input or model knowledge is ambiguous.

stories.title: Attractor States Emerge in Multi-Turn LLM Conversations
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Investigates the long-run dynamics of multi-turn LLM conversations, a poorly understood area in multi-agent settings.
Tests the hypothesis that open-ended LLM discussions settle into 'attractor-like' stable behavioral sets.
Compares self-play and mixed-play debates across 7 LLMs and 20 controversial topics by tracking representation and discourse traits.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Generation and Decision-Making Under Ambiguity
stories.author: Iryna Gurevych
bullet_points: Addresses the critical need for trust and reliability in Large Language Models (LLMs) used in complex, subjective real-world tasks.
Focuses on uncertainty-aware decision-making algorithms, an area less explored compared to model training improvements.
Presents and evaluates new methods to help LLMs handle ambiguity and quantify their own uncertainty during generation.

stories.title: Attractor States Emerge in Multi-Turn LLM Conversations
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Investigates the long-term dynamics of multi-turn LLM conversations in open-ended settings.
Examines whether LLM discussions settle into 'attractor states'—stable, topic-independent behavioral patterns.
Compares self-play and mixed-play debates across 7 LLMs on 20 controversial topics by tracking representation and discourse traits.

stories.title: Morphing into Hybrid Attention Models
stories.author: Yu Cheng
bullet_points: The research proposes hybrid attention models to boost long-context efficiency in NLP.
These models achieve efficiency by substituting full-attention layers with linear attention.
A key challenge identified is that layer selection for conversion currently relies on limited heuristic strategies, ignoring interdependencies.

stories.title: Poller: Are LLMs Suitable for Evaluating the Poetry Understanding Task?
stories.author: Lidia S. Chao
bullet_points: Proposes 'Poller,' a novel method using LLMs to evaluate poetry understanding.
Addresses the limitation of traditional automatic evaluation methods for Chinese poetry.
Aims to replace expensive human evaluation for large-scale literary analysis tasks.

stories.title: Regime-Aware Peer Specialization for Robust RAG under Heterogeneous Knowledge Conflicts
stories.author: Chong Feng
bullet_points: The paper addresses the fragility of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) when retrieved context conflicts with the model's internal knowledge.
It introduces 'Regime-Aware Peer Specialization' to handle heterogeneous knowledge conflicts, which range from reliable to adversarial.
The proposed method aims to overcome limitations of existing, regime-agnostic supervision techniques that conflate incompatible learning signals.

stories.title: SIMAX: A Scalable and Interpretable Framework for Multi-Fidelity and Annotated Clinician-Patient Dialogue Simulation
stories.author: Chuan Hong
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability and cost issues associated with manually coding large volumes of clinician-patient dialogue data.
Introduces SIMAX, a framework designed for multi-fidelity and annotated simulation of clinical conversations.
Focuses on enabling AI-driven communication coding systems by providing necessary evaluation data.

stories.title: Situation Perception: A Necessary Primitive to Artificial Superintelligence
stories.author: Jaymari Chua
bullet_points: The paper argues that current LLMs, while powerful statistical engines, lack true general intelligence.
It posits that 'Situation Perception'—understanding concepts like object permanence and cause/effect—is a necessary primitive for Artificial Superintelligence.
The research contrasts pattern mastery (LLM strength) with embodied, experiential understanding (human infant development).

stories.title: Claude Code is steganographically marking requests
stories.author: kirushik
bullet_points: Claude AI is reportedly embedding hidden data within its requests.
The technique used is steganography, meaning data is concealed within other data.
This raises potential concerns regarding data privacy and model transparency.
